notes: Eval 1 (large-cap-tech): Both configurations pass 13/13 — assertions are non-discriminating. Key quality difference unmeasured: with_skill found IV data (38%, 80%) while old_skill returned N/A for IV Rank/Percentile.

Eval 2 (commodity-producer): ONLY discriminating eval. with_skill produced dedicated '## Gold Commodity Price Analysis (First)' section with quantitative AISC margin calculation ($3,535/oz) and miner leverage math (5% gold = 7.6-8.0% margin change). Old skill mentioned leverage in passing without explanation — correctly failed the assertion.

Eval 3 (options-focused): Both configurations pass 11/11 — both produced excellent options analysis. Old skill added bonus 'Risk Summary for Put Sellers' and 'Put-Selling Specific Checklist' sections not in template. with_skill had more precise IV data from AlphaQuery and 3 named strategy alternatives.

Overall: Improved skill shows measurable advantage on commodity-specific analysis (dedicated section, quantitative leverage, historical valuation averages). For generic large-cap and options-focused prompts, both skills produce comparable quality — the old skill was already strong. Assertions need strengthening to capture qualitative depth differences.

Recommendation: Assertions should be tightened — add checks for numeric IV data (not N/A), historical valuation data completeness, explicit margin/leverage calculations for commodity producers, and multi-strategy options alternatives.
